question: What is the containment vessel design for NuScale?

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates at full power using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for pumps. Its small, modular design integrates the core, steam generators, and pressurizer within the reactor pressure vessel, features passive safety systems not reliant on electrical power, and allows for flexible power maneuvering through control rods and boron concentration adjustments.

nuscale_ch01_0023.title: Containment Systems
nuscale_ch01_0023.summary: The NuScale containment vessel (CNV) is maintained under vacuum during normal operation, which reduces moisture and corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits oxygen for safety. In the event of an emergency, passive cooling rapidly reduces pressure and temperature, and the design’s reliance on passive safety systems and small fuel inventory results in improved safety and a lower core damage frequency compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0168.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.2 Secondary Containment
nuscale_ch01_0168.summary: The NuScale design does not include secondary containment or fission product clean-up systems, as its passive safety features and submerged containment vessel inherently control fission products. Therefore, regulatory criteria related to secondary containment and certain fission product control systems are not applicable to NuScale, and only specific containment parameters such as leakage rates are relevant for review.

nuscale_ch01_0167.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.1 Primary Containment
nuscale_ch01_0167.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to SRP 6.5.3 requirements for primary containment, as its passive containment vessel does not include active fission product clean-up or pressure suppression systems; instead, fission product control is achieved through inherent design features. Criteria related to secondary containment and other fission product control systems are not applicable, since NuScale lacks these features and relies solely on its primary containment vessel and associated isolation systems.

nuscale_ch01_0048.title: Security Buildings
nuscale_ch01_0048.summary: The Security Buildings (SCBs) at a NuScale Power Plant are nonsafety-related structures that include the primary access control building. These buildings support plant security functions but are not classified as safety-related structures within the facility.

nuscale_ch01_0062.title: Control Rod Assembly Drop and Control Rod Drive Shaft Alignment Test
nuscale_ch01_0062.summary: This section describes the testing and validation of the NuScale control rod assembly (CRA) drop and drive shaft alignment, focusing on performance, reliability, and repeatability. Tests conducted at Curtiss Wright facilities demonstrated that the remote disconnect mechanism reliably engages and disengages the CRA, and the results have informed improvements in drive rod position detection circuitry, though they do not form the final design basis. The NPM design features longer, remotely disconnectable drive shafts with multiple alignment supports to ensure proper operation.

nuscale_ch01_0356.title: SRP 9.3.3, Rev 3: Equipment and Floor Drainage SystemII.3 Control of Releases of Radioactive Material to the Environment
nuscale_ch01_0356.summary: The section confirms that the NuScale equipment and floor drainage system meets regulatory requirements for controlling releases of radioactive material to the environment, with no parts of the drain system penetrating the containment barrier. This demonstrates conformance with SRP 9.3.3, Rev 3, ensuring environmental protection from radioactive releases.

nuscale_ch01_0064.title: NuScale Test and Inspection Plans
nuscale_ch01_0064.summary: This section describes NuScale’s test and inspection plans for key reactor components, including control rod drive shafts and Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS) valves. It details how testing confirmed operability and performance under various conditions, and outlines a dedicated test program—conducted at a specialized facility—to demonstrate ECCS valve functionality at reactor operating pressures, temperatures, and in borated coolant, including unique design features like the inadvertent actuation block (IAB).

nuscale_ch01_0050.title: Diesel Generator Buildings
nuscale_ch01_0050.summary: The Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs) in the NuScale Power Plant each house a single backup diesel generator and their auxiliary equipment, but do not contain any safety-related systems or support engineered safety functions (ESFs). Their primary purpose is to provide support and housing for the generators, associated equipment, HVAC systems, and maintenance areas.

nuscale_ch01_0066.title: Material Referenced
nuscale_ch01_0066.summary: This section lists the topical and technical reports referenced in the NuScale Power Plant Design Certification Application, as detailed in Tables 1.6-1 and 1.6-2. These reports cover various aspects such as quality assurance, risk significance, safety classification, accident analysis, and methodology qualifications, and are incorporated by reference into the Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR).

nuscale_ch01_0003.title: Plant Location
nuscale_ch01_0003.summary: This section introduces the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), which is required for standard design certification under 10 CFR 52. It provides an overview of the NuScale Power Module (NPM), a scalable, modular nuclear steam supply system, and summarizes key safety and design features, including passive safety systems, compact containment, and advanced digital controls. The FSAR covers both individual module and multi-module plant configurations.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at the NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references detailed figures and notes that some buildings, like the administration and training building, are outside the scope of the report.

nuscale_ch01_0002.title: Introduction
nuscale_ch01_0002.summary: This section introduces the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), which is submitted as part of the application for standard design certification under U.S. regulations. It outlines the scope of the report, describing the modular NuScale Power Module (NPM) design, its safety features, scalability from one to twelve modules, and key design characteristics such as passive safety systems, compact containment, and advanced digital controls.

nuscale_ch01_0366.title: DSRS 9.3.6, Rev 0: Containment Evacuation and Flooding SystemsII.1 GDC 2
nuscale_ch01_0366.summary: The section summarizes NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for containment evacuation and flooding systems, specifically noting compliance with GDC 2 requirements as outlined in DSRS 9.3.6, Rev 0. It states that the NuScale design meets these criteria with no exceptions or additional comments.

nuscale_ch01_0363.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.7 Prevention of CVCS Holdup Tank Wall Buckling/Failure; CVCS Venting and Draining
nuscale_ch01_0363.summary: The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report states that the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS) generally conforms to regulatory criteria, but only partially meets the requirements for prevention of holdup tank wall buckling/failure because the NuScale design does not include holdup tanks subject to vacuum conditions referenced in the criteria. However, the NuScale CVCS does provide appropriate venting and draining capabilities as required.
